issues.detail: Instructions are descriptive but lack specific, step-by-step guidance for the agent.
Error handling and edge case scenarios are not addressed in the examples.

scenarios.name: Programmatically create a Keycloak user and assign a role
scenarios.rationale: Treatment matches the SKILL.md admin-API pattern: client_credentials token from /realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token, POST to /admin/realms/{REALM}/users with enabled/emailVerified/credentials, then GET /roles/{name} and POST to /users/{id}/role-mappings/realm; baseline misses the role-mappings endpoint shape and uses ad-hoc token fetching.

scenarios.name: Integrate Keycloak SSO into a Next.js app with NextAuth
scenarios.rationale: Treatment uses next-auth/providers/keycloak with issuer built as `${KEYCLOAK_URL}/realms/${KEYCLOAK_REALM}` and persists access/refresh/id tokens in the jwt callback per SKILL.md; baseline uses a generic OAuth setup without the realm-scoped issuer pattern or token persistence callbacks.
